If all three pairs of opposite edges of a tetrahedron are perpendicular , then it is called an orthocentric tetrahedron

An isodynamic tetrahedron is one in which the cevians that join the vertices to the incenters of the opposite faces are concurrent
An isogonic tetrahedron has concurrent cevians that join the vertices to the points of contact of the opposite faces with the inscribed sphere
A disphenoid is a tetrahedron with four congruent triangles as faces
A 3-orthoscheme is a tetrahedron where all four faces are right triangles
